samples.gvd:
module of a carry-lookahead 32-bit adder that uses the Carry-Lookahead Adder (CLA) architecture.

Module name:  
    adder_32bit               
Input ports:
    A[32:1]: 32-bit input operand A.
    B[32:1]: 32-bit input operand B.
Output ports:
    S[32:1]: 32-bit output representing the sum of A and B.
    C32: Carry-out output.

Implementation:
The top module adder_32bit consists of several instances of the 16 bit CLA block you design.

Give me the complete code.

```verilog
module adder_32bit
(
    input [32:1] A,
    input [32:1] B,
    output [32:1] S,
    output C32
);
    wire [15:0] S_lo, S_hi;
    wire C16;

    // Instantiate two 16-bit CLA modules
    cla_16bit cla_lo (
        .A(A[15:1]),
        .B(B[15:1]),
        .Cin(1'b0),
        .S(S_lo),
        .Cout(C16)
    );

    cla_16bit cla_hi (
        .A(A[32:16]),
        .B(B[32:16]),
        .Cin(C16),
        .S(S_hi),
        .Cout(C32)
    );

    // Concatenate the outputs of the two 16-bit CLA modules to form the final 32-bit sum
    assign S = {S_hi, S_lo};

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
Implement a 32-bit carry-lookahead adder using the Carry-Lookahead Adder (CLA) architecture. 32-bit input operand A. 32-bit input operand B. 32-bit output representing the sum of A and B. Carry-out output. 32

Implement a 32-bit carry-lookahead adder using the Carry-Lookahead Adder (CLA) architecture.

For this question, you must use the CLA architecture.

Recall that the CLA architecture uses generate functions to quickly compute carry signals.
